documentation.readme: A fast and multi-purpose DNS toolkit designed for running DNS queries
usage_sections: Features: - Simple and Handy utility to query DNS records. - A, AAAA, CNAME, PTR, NS, MX, TXT, SRV, SOA query support - DNS Resolution / Brute-force support - Custom resolver input support - Multiple resolver format (TCP/UDP/DOH/DOT) support - stdin and stdout support - Automatic wildcard

arguments.name: -auth
arguments.description: configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key (default true)

arguments.name: -auto-wildcard
arguments.description: automatically detect wildcard domains for filtering

arguments.name: -LIMIT
arguments.description: -t, -threads int number of concurrent threads to use (default 100)

arguments.name: -omit-raw
arguments.description: omit raw dns response from jsonl output

arguments.name: -or
arguments.description: omit raw dns response from jsonl output

arguments.name: -silent
arguments.description: display only results in the output

arguments.name: -stats
arguments.description: display stats of the running scan

arguments.name: -stream
arguments.description: stream mode (wordlist, wildcard, stats and stop/resume will be disabled)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the recon category. Fits when the engagement needs: A fast and multi-purpose DNS toolkit designed for running DNS queries.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A fast and multi-purpose DNS toolkit designed for running DNS queries.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: go install -v github.com/projectdiscovery/dnsx/cmd/dnsx@latest. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `go install -v github.com/projectdiscovery/dnsx/cmd/dnsx@latest`. Further invocations from upstream: `dnsx -h`; `subfinder -silent -d hackerone.com | dnsx -silent`. A fast and multi-purpose DNS toolkit designed for running DNS queries.

name: dnsx
summary: A fast and multi-purpose DNS toolkit designed for running DNS queries

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need recon, use dnsx because A fast and multi-purpose DNS toolkit designed for running DNS queries.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `go install -v github.com/projectdiscovery/dnsx/cmd/dnsx@latest` as documented upstream for dnsx.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnsx -h` as documented upstream for dnsx.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `subfinder -silent -d hackerone.com | dnsx -silent` as documented upstream for dnsx.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `subfinder -silent -d hackerone.com | dnsx -silent -a -resp` as documented upstream for dnsx.
When chaining recon tools, pair dnsx with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dnsx --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
